pi_items.source: NCBI/Medical terminology - Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is standard medical diagnosis
NCBI StatPearls: DM and T2DM are standard abbreviations for diabetes mellitus and Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
NCBI StatPearls - Essential Hypertension is a standard medical diagnosis
NCBI/AMBOSS: HTN is the standard medical abbreviation for hypertension; blood pressure is the primary measurement
NCBI StatPearls / Yale Medicine: Major Depressive Disorder is the formal psychiatric diagnosis
NCBI/NAMI: MDD is the standard abbreviation; depression and mood disorder are related clinical terms

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic patient named Maria Santos (DOB 1962-08-14) in a containerized OSCAR EMR test environment. OSCAR is a legitimate open-source electronic medical record system widely used in Canadian healthcare. The three chronic conditions to add are Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (keywords: diabetes, dm, type 2, t2dm), Essential Hypertension (keywords: hypertension, htn, blood pressure), and Major Depressive Disorder (keywords: depress, mdd, mood) - all standard medical diagnoses. The test credentials are oscardoc/oscar/PIN:1117 as configured in the OSCAR test environment setup scripts. All patient and medical data are synthetic, created for testing and educational purposes within a containerized demo environment.
